page: Amazon Web Services c5n.large is a compute-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8124M processor running at 3.4 GHz. The instance provides 2 dedicated vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 5.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding 2.62 GB of memory per core. There is no local storage bundled with this instance, meaning it relies entirely on network-attached storage. Network baseline bandwidth is rated at 3 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate a performance profile that leans towards single-threaded tasks. The single-core Geekbench score of 1133.0 and single-threaded gzip compression of 23.89 GB/sec both rank in the average tier. However, multi-core and memory-intensive benchmarks show limitations. The multi-core Geekbench score of 1345.0 and the stress-ng multi-core CPU performance of 1834.59 fall into the lower percentiles. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with read-only large block bandwidth at 13.56 GB/sec and write large block bandwidth at 11.83 GB/sec.

The c5n.large offers a dedicated hardware profile at a low entry point within the compute-optimized tier, making it a cost-efficient option for workloads requiring dedicated CPU cycles without massive memory demands. The primary tradeoff is the low memory capacity of 5.0 GB and the lack of local storage, which limits its utility for memory-heavy databases or large-scale data processing. It is best suited for lightweight network-bound applications, single-threaded utility scripts, basic web serving, and small-scale computational tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services c5n.large is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance running on the Nitro hypervisor. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8124M processor at 3.4 GHz, it offers 2 dedicated vCPUs and 5.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The instance lacks local storage, relying entirely on network-attached storage, and provides a 3 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks reveal average single-core capabilities, such as a Geekbench single-core score of 1133.0 and single-threaded gzip compression of 23.89 GB/sec, but multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics fall into the lower percentiles. This resource profile represents a qualitative entry-level compute option with dedicated CPU allocation. The c5n.large is best suited for lightweight network-bound workloads, basic web serving, and single-threaded processing tasks that do not require high memory capacity or local storage.

tagline: An entry level dedicated compute instance featuring high network bandwidth designed for lightweight processing and network bound application workloads.
